title: Pockit Help Guide
tagline: How to use Pockit — players, cassettes, doors, and the Family Office stack.

articles.title: What is Pockit?
articles.lede: Pockit is the Family Office player console — a sovereign shell where every app, cassette, and door lives in one grid.
articles.body: Open **http://pockit.localhost/** after `make pockit` (and `make doors` once on a new Mac). The **suite bar** at the top jumps between Pockit, Nephew Control Tower, Automata, Historia, and Voice. The **left rail** lists players and consoles; the **right rail** lists cassettes for the active player. The **center canvas** is where the active tape renders.
steps: Run `make pockit` on the Mac (or `make family` on Spark).
If a door fails, run `make doors` once (sudo Boss Move).
Open http://pockit.localhost/ — not raw LAN ports.
Pick a player in the left rail, then a cassette in the right rail.

articles.title: Doors — friendly URLs
articles.lede: Every tape opens at http://<name>.localhost/ instead of port numbers.
articles.body: The family gateway forwards port 80 to the tape server. **make doors** installs the forwarder (sudo once). **make door** opens the library browser. If a door 502s, run **doors-verify** from the operator console or Help Command Center.
steps: Bookmark http://pockit.localhost/ for daily use.
Cassette doors: http://knowledge.localhost/, http://voice.localhost/, etc.
Never use 192.168.10.205:8790 in operator copy — use doors.

articles.title: First boot checklist
articles.lede: New Mac or fresh clone — run these once before demoing to the family.
articles.body: Register SSH keys so tower-api mints your family session cookie. Rebuild Desktop .apps so double-click launchers exist. Mount the NAS historia share for Visual Obsidian. Then boot Pockit and open the overview grid.
steps: make family-ssh-register
make install-mac-apps (or install-operator-console-app)
make install-nas-mounts (auto-mount /Volumes/historia at login)
make doors (sudo once if prompted)
make pockit → open http://pockit.localhost/
On iPhone: Share → Add to Home Screen for standalone PWA (see Mobile & responsive).

articles.title: Players vs cassettes
articles.lede: A **player** (console) owns cassettes. Pockit is the master player; Nephew CT, Automata, and Dustpan are sibling consoles.
articles.body: Click a player in the **left rail** to filter the right rail. Click a cassette to load it in the center. **Overview** shows every player at once. **Quick Actions** in the suite bar let you pin favorites and drag players or cassettes into slots.
steps: Left rail → pick Automata, Nephew, or Pockit.
Right rail → pick a cassette under that player.
Suite bar brand pill → switch console without leaving Pockit.

articles.title: Rails, footer, and chrome
articles.lede: Collapse rails for focus; the footer shows health, version, and context pills.
articles.body: Drawer handles on the left, right, top, and bottom edges collapse chrome. **Focus mode** and **cinema mode** in Settings → Focus hide rails after idle on desktop; on phone the shell uses the thumb dock instead of edge tabs. Active consoles show an identity badge in the player rail dock.
steps: Players / Cassettes toggles in the toolbar hide rails.
Chrome handle at top collapses suite bar + header.
Footer version pill opens the Pockit changelog modal.
Phone/tablet: see Mobile & responsive — dock, Controls sheet, viewport tiers.

articles.title: Deep links and hash routes
articles.lede: Share a cassette with #/c/<id> in the URL.
articles.body: Examples: http://pockit.localhost/#/c/voice · http://pockit.localhost/#/c/knowledge · http://pockit.localhost/#/welcome for this Help Guide.
steps: Copy the URL from the browser after opening a cassette.
Paste into chat or bookmarks — doors resolve on the same Mac.

articles.title: Multi-surface Pockit
articles.lede: One Family Office shell — phone, tablet, laptop, desktop, TV, Mac .app, and watch companion.
articles.body:
Pockit is **one frame, many shapes**. The same cassettes and doors work on every Apple viewport; the **viewport tier** picks chrome (rails vs thumb dock, footer vs Controls sheet).

## Surfaces

| Surface | Entry | Primary navigation |
|---------|-------|-------------------|
| **Phone** | Safari / PWA | Thumb dock + Controls sheet |
| **Tablet** | iPad Safari | Mobile shell or iPad dashboard rails |
| **Laptop / desktop** | Browser window | Dual rail + suite bar + center footer |
| **Display / TV** | Large window | Dual rail; large canvas |
| **Mac `.app`** | Double-click Desktop app | Boot panel → browser door with weave |
| **Watch** | Companion tier | Minimal card + deep links |

**Operator copy:** always **http://pockit.localhost/** — run `make doors` once on a new Mac (sudo).

Design law: token-first spacing, 44px touch targets on phone, cotton-ball motion, intent chrome on every surface.
steps: Open http://pockit.localhost/ on any device on home LAN.
iPhone: Share → Add to Home Screen for standalone PWA.
Settings → Devices → pick a preset to preview another viewport (session only).
Mac: double-click Pockit.app for boot panel + native weave.

articles.title: Device tiers & viewport matrix
articles.lede: Plan 0275 — nine named tiers from watch through TV, with Device Lab presets and verify gates.
articles.body:
Layout is **predictable at every real Family Office screen size**. Plan 0275 expanded Plan 0263's coarse buckets into **nine tiers** with orientation, device identity, and operator DevTools-style presets.

## Nine tiers (narrow → wide)

`watch` → `phoneCompact` → `phoneLarge` (≤480px) → `tabletCompact` → `tabletLarge` → `laptop` (≤1800) → `desktop` → `display` → `tv`

## Runtime

| Piece | Path |
|-------|------|
| Browser engine | `pockit-viewport.js` — sets `body` classes + data attrs |
| Pure tier logic | `scripts/lib/pockit-viewport-engine.mjs` |
| Preset registry | `data/pockit-device-viewports.json` (+ family-hub copy) |
| Device Lab UI | Settings → **Devices** → preset → Apply (session only) |
| Watch companion | `pockit-watch-companion.js` — minimal UI; phone shell gated off |

## Verify

```bash
node scripts/check-pockit-viewport-matrix.mjs
node containers/nephew-ct/family-hub/pockit-viewport.test.mjs
node scripts/smoke-pockit-ui.mjs
```

Full tier table: **docs/pockit/Device-Viewport-Matrix.md**
steps: Open Settings → Devices in Pockit.
Apply iPhone SE, iPad Pro landscape, MBP 16, or TV 4K preset.
Confirm body tier class matches expected layout.
Run node scripts/check-pockit-viewport-matrix.mjs before ship.

articles.title: Phone shell — dock & Controls
articles.lede: Plan 0274 — thumb dock, mobile HUD, Controls sheet; rails hidden on phone tiers.
articles.body:
On **phoneCompact** and **phoneLarge** (up to **480px**), Pockit switches to **mobile-first phone shell** — not desktop rails squeezed onto a narrow screen.

## What changes on phone

- `body.pockit-phone-shell` — orchestrator class from `pockit-viewport.js`
- **Player + cassette rails collapsed** (width 0); edge drawer handles hidden
- **Center footer hidden** — status moves to **Controls sheet**
- **Bottom thumb dock** — Home · Players · Cassettes · Voice · More (`pockit-phone-dock.js`)
- **Controls FAB / sheet** — reuses footer pill renderers (`pockit-controls-sheet.js`)
- **44px touch targets** + `env(safe-area-inset-*)` padding

## Critical modules

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `pockit-phone-shell.js` | Orchestrator — collapse rails, wire dock |
| `pockit-phone-dock.js` | Bottom navigation |
| `pockit-controls-sheet.js` | Footer + rail controls in sheet |
| `pockit-phone-shell.css` | Layout + HIG hit targets |
| `pockit-rail-drawer.css` | Hides edge tabs on phone |

Voice/Odysseus pads register extra phone chrome via `pockit-phone-player-chrome.js`.
steps: DevTools → iPhone 17 Pro Max or SE — confirm bottom dock visible.
Tap Controls FAB — footer pills and scope appear in sheet.
Confirm no edge rail drawer tabs (~26px) on phone tier.
Real device: http://pockit.localhost/ on home Wi‑Fi.

articles.title: Tablet & iPad dashboard
articles.lede: iPad rails, hub-dashboard theme, and --player-rail-w tokens on tablet tiers.
articles.body:
On **tabletCompact** and **tabletLarge**, Pockit may show the **mobile shell** (dock + Controls) or full **iPad dashboard** rails depending on width and orientation.

## iPad dashboard

`pockit-ipad-dashboard.css` styles the **hub-dashboard** body class — monochrome smart-mirror tiles with SF-style icons while keeping full chrome.

## Rail tokens

| Token | Default | Use |
|-------|---------|-----|
| `--player-rail-w` | 240px | Left player rail |
| `--cassette-rail-w` | 280px class | Right cassette rail |
| `--suite-bar-h` | suite bar height | Top Family Office carousel |

Rails beat Ant Design `!important` overrides via `body.tapes-ui.hub-dashboard .sidebar.player-rail` selectors.

## Tablet vs phone

- Narrow tablet portrait may still use phone shell + dock.
- iPad Pro landscape → dual rails + center footer (like laptop).
- Device Lab → Apply **iPad Pro 13 landscape** to QA without hardware.
steps: Settings → Devices → iPad Pro preset → Apply.
Confirm player rail at --player-rail-w and cassette rail visible.
Rotate — orientation attr updates via pockit-viewport.js.
Overview grid uses hub-dashboard tile styling on iPad.

articles.title: Desktop rails, focus & cinema
articles.lede: Tri-rail shell on laptop/desktop — auto-hide rails from pockit-config after idle.
articles.body:
On **laptop**, **desktop**, **display**, and **tv** tiers Pockit shows the **tri-rail shell**: suite bar, player rail, center canvas + toolbar + footer, cassette rail.

## Chrome controls

| Control | Effect |
|---------|--------|
| Players / Cassettes toggles | Collapse left/right rails |
| Top chrome handle | Collapse suite bar + header |
| Drawer edge tabs | Peek collapsed rails (desktop only) |

## Focus & cinema (`pockit-config.js`)

Settings → **Focus** tab:

- **Focus mode** — after idle, rails auto-hide; move pointer to edge to reveal (`pockit-chrome-reveal-active`)
- **Cinema mode** — forces all chrome hidden for full canvas (`body.pockit-cinema-mode`)

Config persists in `localStorage` via `pockit-config.js` → `chrome.player_rail`, `chrome.cassette_rail`, `chrome.main_header` each with `auto_hide_after_ms` + `collapsed`.

Phone tiers ignore desktop focus idle — they use phone shell instead.
steps: Settings → Focus → enable cinema mode — rails hide immediately.
Disable cinema → set rail auto-hide idle (e.g. 30s) → wait → rails collapse.
Move cursor to left edge — player rail peeks open.
Compare with phone tier — focus idle does not apply there.

articles.title: Touch spacing & hit targets
articles.lede: Understandings 0002/0003 — 44px HIG targets, apple-hig shape law, comet motion.
articles.body:
**Token-first UI** — no ad-hoc pixels or hex in components. Every surface shares `--ak-*` spacing, typography, and radii from `applekit-shell-tokens.css`.

## Minimum touch (phone)

| Control | Min size | Module |
|---------|----------|--------|
| Icon button | 44×44 | `.icon-btn` |
| Dock tab | 44px height | `pockit-phone-dock.js` |
| Voice Talk | 44px | `voice-pad.css` |
| Shield / intent | 44×44 | `.fo-proto-shield` |
| Primary CTA | 32–44px | `--ak-control-height-md` |

## Shape law

- **Rounded rectangles** for buttons, chips, status — `--ak-radius-sm` through `--ak-radius-xl`
- **`--ak-radius-pill` / 999px** only for LEDs, avatars, segmented tracks — **not** status chips
- Gate: `node scripts/check-pockit-hig-shape.mjs`

## Motion

**Cotton-ball settle** (`comet-motion.css`) — panels and sheets sneeze-out with soft overshoot; respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.

8pt grid: `--ak-space-1` (4px) through `--ak-space-6` (24px). Phone adds `--pockit-phone-dock-h`, `--pockit-phone-chrome-bottom`, safe-area insets.
steps: DevTools phone tier — tap smallest footer/dock control; target ≥44px.
Run node scripts/check-pockit-hig-shape.mjs before UI ship.
Confirm status chips use --ak-radius-sm, not pill stadiums.
Toggle reduced motion in OS — panels fade without overshoot.

articles.title: PWA — Add to Home Screen
articles.lede: Plan 0280 — iPhone Home Screen standalone icon with phone shell and last-route memory.
articles.body:
Pockit ships as a **Progressive Web App** for iPhone and iPad Safari.

## Onboard (once per device)

1. On home Wi‑Fi, open **http://pockit.localhost/** in Safari (requires `make doors` on the Mac serving the gateway).
2. Tap **Share** → **Add to Home Screen**.
3. Confirm name **Pockit** and add.
4. Launch from Home Screen — opens **standalone** (no Safari chrome) with phone shell.

## What ships

| Asset | Role |
|-------|------|
| `pockit.webmanifest` | Standalone display, theme color, icons |
| Apple meta tags | `apple-mobile-web-app-capable`, status bar style |
| Icons | Home Screen icon set in family-hub |
| Route memory | `localStorage` remembers last `#/c/…` route |

Deep links (`#/c/voice`, `#/c/knowledge`) survive PWA launch. Same SSO cookie rules as browser — register SSH keys first (`make family-ssh-register`).
steps: make doors (sudo once) + make pockit on the Mac.
Safari → http://pockit.localhost/ → Share → Add to Home Screen.
Launch standalone icon — confirm bottom dock, no Safari URL bar.
Navigate to a cassette, close app, reopen — last route restores.

articles.title: Mac app vs browser
articles.lede: Native weave, boot panel, and isNativePockitHubSurface — when UI differs from Safari.
articles.body:
## Mac `.app` path

Double-click **Pockit.app** (or sibling Family Office apps):

```text
Boot panel (intent shield, LED steps, version footer)
  → repo sync + Desktop apps refresh (Pockit only)
  → gateway → stack → pave routes → SSO
  → readiness probe → open browser (?mac_app=pockit)
```

Plans: **0278** tap-to-open · **0283–0285** intent chrome weave.

## Browser path

Bookmark or PWA **http://pockit.localhost/** — page load, no boot panel. Mac weave tiles still appear in cassette rail when launched with `?mac_app=` param.

## When UI differs

| Concern | Mac `.app` | Browser |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| Launcher | Swift + LED boot panel | URL / PWA |
| Intent chrome | Boot panel + in-browser shield | Weave + registry inject |
| Native hub surfaces | `isNativePockitHubSurface()` — voice, knowledge, overview, library render full-page HTML, never iframe embed trap | Same |
| Type B HTML | Local server surfaces (console.localhost) | Same via doors |
| Phone shell | N/A at boot; in-browser tier still applies | Tier from viewport |

`isNativePockitHubSurface(cassette)` in `pockit.js` returns true for voice, knowledge, odysseus, overview, library, smoke-checklist, suite-welcome, and encompass native pads.
steps: make install-mac-apps — Desktop double-click launchers.
Double-click Pockit.app — watch boot panel LEDs → browser opens.
Compare with http://pockit.localhost/ in Safari — same shell, no boot panel.
Open Voice pad — native surface fills center canvas (not iframe).

articles.title: Mobile troubleshooting
articles.lede: Wrong tier, stale cache, viewport matrix failures — how to diagnose layout surprises.
articles.body:
## Wrong layout tier

Symptom: desktop rails on iPhone, or dock missing on phone.

1. Hard refresh — Shift-reload in Safari or kill PWA and reopen.
2. Settings → Devices — clear lab override (session preset stuck).
3. Check `body` classes: `pockit-phone-shell`, `pockit-viewport-tier-*` in DevTools.
4. Run `node scripts/check-pockit-viewport-matrix.mjs` — registry ↔ engine parity.

## Stale assets / version lie

Footer version should match CHANGELOG. Hard refresh http://pockit.localhost/. If footer shows old pin after merge, `git pull` on host serving family-hub.

## PWA won't install

- Confirm **http://pockit.localhost/** loads (not LAN IP).
- Run `make doors` once (sudo Boss Move).
- Must be on home Wi‑Fi or VPN with gateway reachable.

## Smoke gates

```bash
node scripts/smoke-pockit-ui.mjs
node scripts/check-pockit-viewport-matrix.mjs
node scripts/check-pockit-hig-shape.mjs
```

## Device Lab QA

Settings → Devices → Apply **iPhone SE** then **iPhone 17 Pro Max** — both should show phone shell (Pro Max uses phoneLarge ≤480px).
steps: Shift-reload http://pockit.localhost/.
node scripts/smoke-pockit-ui.mjs from ~/Developer/nephew.
node scripts/check-pockit-viewport-matrix.mjs if tier logic changed.
Real device on Wi‑Fi — confirm dock + Controls sheet.

articles.title: Knowledge — Brain A chat
articles.lede: Grounded RAG chat over rules, plans, Clinic, Historia, and the sovereign vault.
articles.body: Knowledge pad uses Brain A on Spark (Qdrant + bge-m3). Scope picker in the left rail limits retrieval domains. Right rail shows brain health and reindex commands. **Nephew/Understandings** docs sync to the sovereign vault and index into Brain A after `make vault-ingest-docs`.
steps: make corpus-status on Spark to see shelf fill.
Open Knowledge → pick scope → ask a question.
After doc edits: make vault-ingest-docs (Mac vault sync + Spark Qdrant index).
Reindex with make index-corpus when shelves are stale.

articles.title: Versions and changelog
articles.lede: One Pockit version pill in the footer — opens a centered changelog modal.
articles.body: Pockit version is pinned in pockit-surface.json and synced across the monorepo. Click the footer pill to read what shipped. Active console cassettes show identity in the rail dock, not a second changelog.
steps: Footer → tap Pockit v… pill.
Read entries with letter line IDs (e.g. 1.88.0.a).
Agents: always read version from disk before editing.

articles.title: Super Rick — hybrid Mac + DGX architecture
articles.lede: Super Rick split stack — edge STT/TTS on Mac, premium brain and voices on Spark.
articles.body:
**Super Rick** (cassette id `voice`) runs as a **split stack** — edge on the Mac, premium on nephew-spark. *Parakeet* is legacy Plan 0201 internal naming only.

## Layers

```text
Mic → Whisper STT (M5 or DGX)
    → tower-api /api/v1/voice/turn
    → Processors (turn-taking · retrieval cache · emotion swarmer · double-pass · emotion-aware)
         └─ emotion swarmer may delegate swarm merge → seed processor HTTP sidecar (:8097)
    → Brain (Ollama qwen on Spark or fast model on Mac)
    → Optional RAG (Qdrant /api/v1/retrieve)
    → TTS (Holler M5 · Higgs v3 DGX · Spark-TTS · F5 clone · NeMo Riva · Kokoro ops-only)
    → Speakers
```

## Seed processor HTTP sidecar (Plan 0294 P7)

tower-api remains the orchestrator. Pipecat seed logic in `docs/super-rick-seed/custom_processors/` is exposed as a **fleet cassette** — not a second voice UI:

| Piece | Path / port |
|-------|-------------|
| Fleet cassette | `deploy/dgx/fleet/super-rick-seed-processors/` |
| Health | `GET http://127.0.0.1:8097/health` (Spark loopback) · `http://10.1.0.5:8097` (mesh) |
| tower-api client | `src/voice/seed-sidecar-client.js` |
| Config | `data/voice-config.json` → `seed_sidecars` (`fallback_js: true`) |

When the sidecar is down, `emotion-swarmer` falls back to `src/voice/swarm-merge.js` — voice keeps working.

**Mac Colima:** `deploy/dgx/fleet/docker-compose.mac-edge.yml` binds **127.0.0.1:8097** only (Darwin cannot bind `10.1.0.5`).

**Verify:** `make ensure-super-rick-seed-sidecar` · `make smoke-super-rick-seed-sidecar` · Pockit **v1.91.99**

## UI layers (parallel — not replacements)

| Layer | Path | Role |
|-------|------|------|
| Pockit pad | `#/c/voice` · `voice-pad.js` | Operator console |
| The Presence | `apps/super-rick-presence/` | Cinematic TSL/SDF orb (Plan 0429) |
| voice projection | http://voice.localhost/ | Standalone console door |

## Route advisor

| Route | When |
|-------|------|
| **Auto** | Healthy M5 edge preferred; DGX when edge down |
| **M5** | Pin STT + Holler to this Mac — lowest latency |
| **DGX** | Premium voices + larger models on Spark |

## Sovereign law

No cloud STT/TTS for user-facing Talk. Kokoro on DGX is **ops/health probe only** — never user-facing speak per Voice Quality Covenant (`quality_covenant` in voice-config.json).

## Docs

- **Minimum specs & iPhone performance** — Help article `voice-system-specs` · [`standard-voice-stack/understandings/Voice-System-Specs-and-iPhone-Performance.md`](https://git.jailynmarvin.com/marvelousempire/standard-voice-stack/src/branch/main/understandings/Voice-System-Specs-and-iPhone-Performance.md)
- Understandings: `Nephew/Understandings/Voice/understandings/PRIVATE VOICE-FIRST AI SYSTEM — COMP.md` (submodule)
- Plan 0294 Super Rick bridge · Plan 0429 The Presence · Plan 0201 Parakeet cassette

articles.title: voice-config.json reference
articles.lede: Field-by-field map of the live voice routing config.
articles.body:
Canonical file: `data/voice-config.json` — edit via **Configurations Center** or **Settings → Voice** (tower-api PATCH). Schema: `data/schema-library/schemas/voice-config.schema.json`.

## Top-level keys

| Field | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `schema_version` | Config format (currently `2`) |
| `quality_covenant` | No cloud TTS, no browser speech, Kokoro ops-only |
| `default_voice` | Persona id (e.g. `jarvis`) |
| `voice_aliases` | Short names → persona ids |
| `default_model` / `fast_model` / `prime_model` | Ollama model refs for chat turns |
| `engines` | TTS engine registry (`holler`, `f5-tts`, `spark-tts`, …) |
| `personas` | Speaker roster — voice, engine, tier, group |
| `presets` / `kokoro_presets` / `fallback_presets` | Engine-specific voice presets |
| `semantic_cache` | Edge cache policy |
| `emotion_swarmer` | 4-agent parallel merge before double-pass |
| `seed_sidecars` | Plan 0294 P7 — HTTP delegate to `:8097` seed processor cassette |
| `turn_emotion` | Spark-TTS emotion routing |

### `seed_sidecars` (P7)

| Field | Default | Meaning |
|-------|---------|---------|
| `enabled` | `true` | Try HTTP sidecar before JS merge |
| `fallback_js` | `true` | Use `swarm-merge.js` when sidecar unreachable |
| `use_merge` | `true` | Delegate swarm merge to `POST /v1/processors/swarm/merge` |
| `use_emotion_aware` | `false` | Optional `POST /v1/processors/emotion-aware` |
| `port` | `8097` | Fleet cassette port |
| `local_url` | `http://127.0.0.1:8097` | Loopback probe (Spark + Mac) |
| `timeout_ms` | `800` | Sidecar fetch budget |

Override with env `NEPHEW_SEED_SIDECAR_URL` for smoke/debug.

## Write policy

- **tower-api:** `PATCH /api/v1/operator/config/voice-config` (operator session, localhost)
- **read-only in git:** mac-app-intent-registry — change via PR

## Verify

`node scripts/validate-pockit-configuration-registry.mjs` — registry + schema gate.

articles.title: Voice make targets
articles.lede: launchagent, ensure-voice, smoke, and deploy commands from HCC.
articles.body:
Run from Nephew repo on Mac or Spark. Full catalog: http://console.localhost/ → **Super Rick Voice** section (HCC id `parakeet-voice` — legacy catalog key).

## Daily

| Target | When |
|--------|------|
| `make pockit` | Boot Pockit player + gateway |
| `make voice-launchagent` | Always-on Voice edge on Mac |
| `make ensure-voice` | Heal DGX premium voice stack |
| `make ensure-m5-voice` | Heal M5 Holler + Whisper |
| `make install-voice-app` | Desktop Voice.app accessory |

## Smoke / verify

| Target | When |
|--------|------|
| `make smoke-voice` | End-to-end voice health |
| `make ensure-super-rick-seed-sidecar` | Start/rebuild seed processor HTTP sidecar (:8097) |
| `make smoke-super-rick-seed-sidecar` | P7 doctor + swarm merge smoke |
| `make cassette-line CHECK=voice` | Cassette assembly line |
| `make cartridge-accessory-line CHECK=voice` | Voice.app accessory gate |

## Deploy

| Target | When |
|--------|------|
| `make deploy-voice-premium-dgx` | Premium TTS on Spark |
| `make ensure-spark-tts-weights-m5` | Native Spark-TTS 0.5B weights |

Click ▶ on the Voice pad **Deploy** rail to run make targets via tower-api (operator session required).

articles.title: Ports, doors, health probes
articles.lede: Friendly door URLs and tower-api probes — never open :8088 as a UI.
articles.body:
**Operator copy:** always use **http://<id>.localhost/** — run `make doors` once on a new Mac (sudo).

## Voice doors

| Door | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| http://pockit.localhost/#/c/voice | Voice pad in Pockit shell (ops + Console \| Presence tabs) |
| http://voice.localhost/ | **The Presence** cinematic orb — canonical door (v0.3.10+: ⚙ settings first) |
| http://voice.localhost/presence | Same orb — legacy/embed alias (Pockit iframe paths) |
| http://voice.localhost/console | Voice ops dashboard (health tiles, Make grid) |
| http://voice.localhost/super-rick | Super Rick bridge LED table |

## The Presence (dev / Docker)

| Surface | URL |
|---------|-----|
| Local dev | http://127.0.0.1:8810/ |
| After `docs/super-rick-presence-seed/plant-seed.sh` | http://127.0.0.1:8810/ |

Sovereign bind: **127.0.0.1:8810** only — not LAN `0.0.0.0`.

## API (not a browser UI)

| Port | Service |
|------|---------|
| **8088** | tower-api — `/api/v1/voice/*` JSON only |
| **8810** | The Presence Next.js UI (slice 1) |

Never open `http://127.0.0.1:8088` expecting a web app.

## Debug (gateway port)

| Probe | Command |
|-------|---------|
| Voice health | `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/v1/voice/health` |
| Super Rick | `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/v1/voice/super-rick/status` |
| The Presence | `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:8810/` |

## Fleet sidecars (DGX / mesh)

| Port | Service | Probe |
|------|---------|-------|
| **8092** | Spark-TTS Holler bridge | `make ensure-spark-tts-weights-m5` when native weights missing |
| **8093** | Spark-TTS native (M5 weights) | same |
| **8095** | Higgs v3 emotional TTS | `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8095/health` |
| **8096** | NeuTTS Air clone TTS | `make smoke-neutts-air` |
| **8097** | Super Rick seed processors (P7) | `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8097/health` |

**8097 notes**

- Spark: bind `127.0.0.1:8097` + `10.1.0.5:8097` — tower-api on Linux uses loopback.
- Mac: overlay `docker-compose.mac-edge.yml` — **127.0.0.1:8097** only.
- Do not curl `10.1.0.5:8097` from Spark itself (hairpin timeout); use loopback for local checks.
- Smoke script extension is **`.mjs`** — `node scripts/smoke-super-rick-seed-sidecar.mjs`.

articles.title: The Presence — cinematic orb UI
articles.lede: Super Rick's voice-first cinematic surface — TSL/SDF orb at 127.0.0.1:8810.
articles.body:
**The Presence** is Super Rick's voice-first cinematic surface — one living 3D orb in near-black space, not a chat website.

Plan: [`plans/0429-super-rick-presence-ui.md`](../../../plans/0429-super-rick-presence-ui.md) · COMP §10 · Bridge Plan Phase 7.

## Surfaces

| Surface | URL | Status |
|---------|-----|--------|
| Dev (Mac) | http://127.0.0.1:8810/ | ✅ slice 1 — `apps/super-rick-presence/` |
| Docker plant | http://127.0.0.1:8810/ | ✅ `docs/super-rick-presence-seed/plant-seed.sh` |
| Pockit console pad | http://pockit.localhost/#/c/voice | ✅ unchanged — ops console (`voice-pad.js`) |
| Projection door | http://voice.localhost/ | ✅ slice 2 — voice-console embed (Pockit pad iframes by default) |
| Bridge LED table | http://voice.localhost/super-rick | ✅ ops status (not the orb) |

## Stack

- **Next.js 15** + React Three Fiber + Three.js
- **Production orb:** TSL **SDF raymarch** — fbm interior, Henyey–Greenstein scatter, emotion palette uniforms
- **Prototype orb:** GLSL icosahedron (`Orb.tsx`) — Shader Park / ShaderToy reference path kept for A/B
- **Renderer:** WebGPU when available; WebGL 2 fallback with Bloom/Vignette postprocessing
- **Voice reactivity:** Web Audio RMS/bass/mid/treble → shader uniforms; states `idle | listening | thinking | speaking`

Backend stays **tower-api** + Bridge Plan processors — slice 2 proxies `/api/v1/voice/turn` and maps Emotion Swarmer tags to orb colors.

## Controls (v0.3.10+)

On **`http://voice.localhost/`** the chrome dock is **voice-first**:

| Button | Panel |
|--------|--------|
| **⚙ Voice settings** | Route, RAG lane, delivery style, **Turn-taking** (Conversation / Tap each turn / Auto-relisten), STT/Prime/Memory/MCP |
| **◎ Dials, haptics & tweaks** | Playground hood — wow presets, scatter/orbit/compass motion (not voice session settings) |

Settings sync with Pockit **`http://pockit.localhost/#/c/voice`** via `tower-cassette-settings-voice`. See [`Super-Rick-Conversation-Mode.md`](../../Super-Rick-Conversation-Mode.md).

## Dev

```bash
cd apps/super-rick-presence && pnpm install && pnpm dev
```

Open http://127.0.0.1:8810/

## Docker seed

```bash
chmod +x docs/super-rick-presence-seed/plant-seed.sh
./docs/super-rick-presence-seed/plant-seed.sh
```

Family Office law: compose binds **127.0.0.1:8810** on the host — not LAN `0.0.0.0`.

## Docs

- Shader notes: `docs/super-rick-presence/sdf-raymarch-notes.md`
- COMP: `Nephew/Understandings/Voice/understandings/PRIVATE VOICE-FIRST AI SYSTEM — COMP.md`

articles.title: Route · Mode · RAG · Delivery
articles.lede: Controller rail controls — how M5 vs DGX routing works in the pad.
articles.body:
These match the Voice pad **Controller** rail (left side). See `voice-rail-info.js` in-repo for LED copy.

## Route

- **Auto** — advisor picks M5 or DGX when healthy
- **M5 edge** — Holler + Whisper on this Mac
- **DGX** — premium Kokoro / Spark-TTS / large models on Spark

Footer **Auto** pill mirrors route preference (stored in pad session + voice-config defaults).

## Mode

- **Chat** — full conversational turns with history
- **Read aloud** — TTS without starting a chat turn
- **Prime** — largest models + deep RAG

## RAG

- **Fast RAG** — skip retrieve for snappier replies
- **Grounded RAG** — query Qdrant / family corpus before brain

## Delivery (Speakers)

Opens persona roster — Jarvis, Warm host, News anchor shortcuts. Personas defined in `voice-config.json` → `personas[]`.

Change **default route** persistently in **Settings → Voice** or Configurations Center.

articles.title: Stack map (STT · brain · TTS · cache)
articles.lede: What each layer does — mirrors the Voice pad Stack rail.
articles.body:
Live labels mirror the Voice pad **Stack** rail (right side).

## M5 edge (this Mac)

| Layer | Service | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| STT | Whisper via tower-api | `make ensure-m5-voice` |
| TTS premium | Holler (Qwen3-TTS 6-bit) | Route `m5` in voice-config engines |
| TTS interim | Kokoro M5 | Until Holler/Python path fully unified |

## DGX Spark (nephew-spark)

| Layer | Service | Port (debug) |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| Brain | Ollama qwen2.5 | 11434 (mesh) |
| Processors (HTTP) | Super Rick seed sidecar (P7) | **8097** — swarm merge delegate |
| TTS premium | Higgs v3, NeuTTS Air, F5-TTS, NeMo Riva, Spark-TTS | 8095 / 8096 / 7860 / 9001 / 8092–8093 |
| TTS fallback | Kokoro DGX | ops only |
| RAG | Qdrant + bge-m3 + reranker | 6333 / 9200 |

## Semantic cache

`voice-config.json` → `semantic_cache` — dedupe similar turns on edge before hitting brain.

## Health probes

Green LEDs on the Stack rail = tower-api answered recently. Run **make smoke-voice** or open Ship Integrity for fleet drift.

articles.title: Super Rick — what it is
articles.lede: The sovereign voice-first persona — 100% local on Mac M5 + DGX Spark.
articles.body:
**Super Rick** is the family voice-first AI persona on sovereign hardware — not a cloud assistant.

## Intent (from mac-app-intent-registry)

- **Why:** We built Super Rick because we own top-tier hardware and refused robotic, laggy, or cloud-tethered voice — the fastest, smoothest, most emotionally intelligent fully private voice agent on Earth, 100% local with zero data leaving the house.
- **Problem:** Cloud assistants leak data, sound flat, and lose context on fast messy brainstorms.
- **Does:** Hybrid Mac M5 + DGX Spark — Fish Audio S2 Pro + Higgs v3, NeuTTS Air, Redis + Atlas memory, Emotion Swarmer, Pipecat v1.4, P-EAGLE-3 speculative decode.

Open the **Intent** pill on `#/c/voice` for the full master document: success criteria + why guide table (COMP v4.0.3).

## Minimum specs

Hardware, software, settings, and **why iPhone feels fast** (auto-route + fast lane + streaming TTS): Help → **Minimum specs & iPhone performance**.

## Surfaces

| Surface | URL | Role |
|---------|-----|------|
| Voice pad (console view) | http://pockit.localhost/#/c/voice | Ops console — Talk, rails, deploy |
| **The Presence** (door) | http://voice.localhost/ | Cinematic orb + live turn cycle |
| Voice projection door | http://voice.localhost/ | Voice console projection |
| Super Rick status | http://voice.localhost/super-rick | COMP bridge LED table |
| Voice.app accessory | `make install-voice-app` | Desktop accessory |

**The Presence** (`apps/super-rick-presence/`) does **not** replace the Pockit pad — it is the cinematic layer. See Help article **The Presence — cinematic orb UI**.

## North-star docs

| Doc | Role |
|-----|------|
| COMP v4.0.3 | Full target spec + seeds |
| Bridge Plan | Super Rick COMP bridge via tower-api (Phases 1–5 + 7 ✅; evolved from legacy Parakeet pad) |
| Plan 0429 | The Presence UI slices |
| `docs/super-rick-seed/` | Pipecat Supernova propagation |
| `docs/super-rick-presence-seed/` | Presence Docker plant |

## Success criteria (operator)

- Almost no noticeable delay in normal conversation
- Handles fast, messy brainstorms without dropping context
- 100% local — no data leaves the home network
- Smooth, expressive voice — not robotic TTS
- **The Presence:** orb reacts to voice + emotion (slice 2 wires live turns)

Open **Configurations Center** (`#/c/configurations`) for live `voice-config.json` or change defaults in **Settings → Voice**.

articles.title: Minimum specs & iPhone performance
articles.lede: Hardware, software, settings, and why auto-route + fast lane feels instant on iPhone.
articles.body:
What hardware and software the Family Office voice stack needs, which settings control routing, and **why iPhone Safari feels fast** — auto-adapt to the device in front of you and the server behind tower-api when edge is not local.

## Minimum hardware

| Role | Minimum | Recommended | What it runs |
|------|---------|-------------|--------------|
| **M5 edge Mac** | Apple Silicon Mac with ANE | **Mac M5 Max** (Family Office edge) | Whisper STT, Holler / Spark-TTS, semantic cache tunnel, `m5-hardware.json` probe |
| **DGX Spark** | NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GB10 node on mesh | **nephew-spark** (`10.1.0.5`) | Ollama / vLLM brain, F5-TTS, NeMo Riva, Kokoro ops probe, Qdrant RAG |
| **Operator phone** | iPhone with Safari 17+ | Same, on home Wi‑Fi or WireGuard mesh | **The Presence** orb UI — WebGL 2 path; voice turns via tower-api proxy |
| **Network** | WireGuard peer or LAN to gateway host | Mesh + `make doors` on the Mac that runs the gateway | Friendly doors (`http://voice.localhost/`) — never memorize ports |

**iPhone is a thin client.** It does not run STT, TTS, or LLM weights locally. Speed comes from **fast-lane API contracts** (below), **streaming audio**, **route auto**, and **cache hits** — not from running models on the phone.

## Minimum software

| Layer | Requirement |
|-------|-------------|
| **tower-api** | Running on the gateway Mac or reachable over mesh (`make ensure-tower-api`) |
| **M5 voice edge** | `make ensure-m5-voice` or `make voice-launchagent` (reboot-safe) |
| **DGX voice** | `make ensure-dgx-voice` when Spark carries premium TTS / brain |
| **Redis STM** | Semantic + audio cache (`make ensure-redis-stm`) — sub-300ms repeats |
| **Presence seed** | `make presence-seed` — orb at `http://voice.localhost/` |
| **Doors** | `make doors` once per Mac (sudo) — clean `http://<name>.localhost/` URLs |

Verify: `make smoke-voice` · `make voice-quality-gate` · Voice pad **Ship Integrity**.

## Auto-adapt routing (client + server)

### 1 — Browser host detection

`voice-system-advisor.js` reads **User-Agent** and optional `http://127.0.0.1:9876/m5-hardware.json`:

- **iPhone / iPad** → `isLocalEdge: false` — Holler on this Mac is not available; routing leans **DGX** when healthy.
- **Mac on the M5 edge** → prefers **M5** when Whisper + TTS probes are green.
- **Remote browser** (not on edge Mac) → same as phone: server-side route resolution matters most.

The Voice pad **Route · Mode** rail and advisor modals explain *why* a route was picked.

### 2 — tower-api `resolveRoute()` (authoritative)

On every STT / TTS / turn, tower-api probes M5 Whisper + M5 TTS:

| Request header / body | Behavior |
|-----------------------|----------|
| `X-Voice-Route: auto` (default) | **M5** when both edge probes OK; else **DGX** |
| `X-Voice-Route: m5` | Pin edge; fall back to DGX if edge down |
| `X-Voice-Route: dgx` | Pin Spark path |

Response headers echo the pick: `X-Voice-Route`, `X-Voice-Engine`, `X-Voice-Fast`.

### 3 — Presence settings + fast lane (iPhone + orb)

The Presence orb reads **`tower-cassette-settings-voice`** (same key as the Pockit voice pad and cassette Settings). Open the **◦** panel on **http://voice.localhost/** for Route, RAG lane, delivery style, Prime, Memory, and MCP — changes sync across tabs and Pockit.

| Setting | Default | Effect |
|---------|---------|--------|
| **RAG lane · Fast** | yes (cassette default) | `X-Voice-Fast: 1`, no corpus retrieve — fastest replies |
| **RAG lane · Grounded** | operator toggle | Client prefetches `/api/v1/retrieve`, then **fast stream** with `rag_context` injected (no second server retrieve wait) |
| **Route** | `auto` | `X-Voice-Route: auto` \| `m5` \| `dgx` |
| **Delivery style** | warm | Maps to persona voice (`nephew` / `jarvis` / `board`) |

```text
POST /api/v1/voice/turn/stream
Headers: X-Voice-Agent: presence, X-Voice-Route: <settings>, X-Voice-Rag: fast|grounded, X-Voice-Fast: 1 when fast lane
Body: { fast, grounded, stream_audio: true, channel: "presence", rag_context?: "…" }
```

What that buys you on iPhone:

| Mechanism | Effect |
|-----------|--------|
| **`fast: true` + `X-Voice-Fast: 1`** | Shorter TTS timeout, skips heavy double-pass audit when policy allows (`skip_on_fast` in voice-config) |
| **Grounded + client prefetch** | Family corpus in the reply without blocking the stream on a second server retrieve |
| **`stream_audio: true`** | SSE `audio` events — playback starts before the full WAV is ready |
| **`channel: "presence"`** | Semantic cache namespace `voice-fast` on fast lane — repeat questions hit Redis before LLM |
| **`X-Voice-Route: auto`** | Spark carries STT/TTS/brain when the phone is not on the M5 edge Mac |

STT from the mic uses the same auto route: `POST /api/v1/voice/stt` with `X-Voice-Route: auto`.

### 4 — Orb renderer adapts to GPU

`PresenceScene.tsx` tries **WebGPU** first; **iPhone Safari** uses **WebGL 2** + postprocessing (Bloom/Vignette). The orb stays 60fps-class without desktop-only APIs.

Audio reactivity uses Web Audio RMS → shader uniforms (`idle | listening | thinking | speaking`).

## Key settings (`data/voice-config.json`)

Edit via **Configurations Center** or **Settings → Voice** (tower-api PATCH). Schema: `data/schema-library/schemas/voice-config.schema.json`.

| Area | Fields | iPhone / fast-turn notes |
|------|--------|--------------------------|
| **Models** | `fast_model`, `prime_model`, `double_pass` | Fast turns use `nephew:fast`; prime audit skipped on fast + cache hit |
| **Engines** | `engines.*.route` (`m5` / `dgx`) | Holler + Spark-TTS → `m5`; F5/Riva → `dgx` |
| **Semantic cache** | `semantic_cache.enabled`, `namespace: voice-fast` | Repeat queries <300ms when Redis STM healthy |
| **Audio cache** | `audio_cache.enabled` | SSE stream replays cached WAV chunks on exact repeat |
| **Quality covenant** | `quality_covenant` | No cloud TTS; Kokoro **ops-only** — never user-facing speak |

Full field reference: Help article **voice-config.json reference**.

## Performance checklist (iPhone feels fast)

1. **Mesh or home Wi‑Fi** — phone reaches the Mac gateway running tower-api + doors.
2. **`make ensure-voice`** on the edge Mac — M5 STT/TTS + LaunchAgent healthy.
3. **`make ensure-redis-stm`** — semantic cache not silently missing (`configured: true, ok: true`).
4. **Open** `http://voice.localhost/` (not raw `:8810` unless debugging).
5. **Hard refresh** after deploy — orb + client JS cache-busted with Pockit version.
6. **DevPanel** (orb footer, dev builds) — confirm route header `m5` or `dgx`, `cache_hit`, `elapsed_ms`.

When edge is down, iPhone still works — **DGX** carries the turn; latency rises but the same fast-lane headers apply.

## Where to read more

| Doc | Path |
|-----|------|
| Stack map | Help → **Stack map** |
| M5 vs DGX routing | Help → **Route · Mode · RAG · Delivery** |
| Presence orb UI | Help → **The Presence** |
| Repo deep dive | `standard-voice-stack/understandings/Voice-System-Specs-and-iPhone-Performance.md` |
| Voice ledger | `Nephew/Understandings/Voice/understandings/Super-Rick-Voice-Stack-Ledger.md` |

## Operator commands

| Target | When |
|--------|------|
| `make export-pockit-help-voice` | After editing these Help articles |
| `make smoke-voice` | End-to-end voice health |
| `make presence-seed` | Rebuild orb Docker door |
| `make ensure-voice` | Full M5 + DGX voice stack heal |

Help Command Center → section **Super Rick Voice** (HCC `parakeet-voice` — legacy catalog key).

articles.title: Voice troubleshooting
articles.lede: Mic, tower-api, TTS covenant, tunnels, and door URL mistakes.
articles.body:
## Mic / permissions (Boss Move)

macOS **Privacy → Microphone** must allow the browser or Voice.app. TCC dialogs require human approval.

## tower-api down

Symptom: Stack rail all red, Talk does nothing.

1. `make pockit` or `make family` on the host running tower-api
2. `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/v1/voice/health` (debug)
3. Check Ship Integrity pad for Spark SSH probe drift

## Text-only mode (no speak)

Voice Quality Covenant may force text when premium engines down. Run `make ensure-m5-voice` and `make ensure-voice`. Kokoro must **not** be used for user Talk — fix Holler/Spark-TTS instead.

## Tunnel / off-LAN

SSH tunnel 8088 alone does not show a UI. Tunnel 8790 or use WireGuard + door URLs after `make doors`.

## Wrong door URL

Use http://voice.localhost/ — not LAN IP `:8790` in operator copy. Clinic **0015** if an agent cites gateway ports.

## Seed processor sidecar down (:8097)

Symptom: Super Rick LED row **Seed processor HTTP sidecar** is yellow; `curl http://127.0.0.1:8097/health` fails.

Voice **still works** — `seed_sidecars.fallback_js` routes merge back to JS (`swarm-merge.js`).

1. `make ensure-super-rick-seed-sidecar` (rebuilds image before `up`)
2. `curl -sf --max-time 3 http://127.0.0.1:8097/health` → `{"ok":true,...}`
3. `node scripts/smoke-super-rick-seed-sidecar.mjs` (`.mjs` not `.mis`)
4. Mac only: confirm `docker-compose.mac-edge.yml` overlay is in use (Darwin cannot bind `10.1.0.5:8097`)
5. `docker logs nephew-fleet-super-rick-seed-processors-1 --tail 40` if health never comes up

Branch: `feat/super-rick-seed-p7` · Pockit **v1.91.99**

## Still stuck

`make smoke-voice` · Clinic cases under voice/parakeet · `#/c/ship-integrity` for pad visibility gates.

articles.title: Hybrid Mac + DGX video architecture
articles.lede: Prompt processors → Mac draft → Visual Swarmer → DGX final → stream.
articles.body:
Split stack — Mac fast drafts, DGX cinematic finals.

## Layers

```text
Prompt + refs → tower-api /api/v1/video/render
    → PromptRefiner → ReferenceImage → DraftVideoGenerator (Mac)
    → Visual Consistency Swarmer (4 agents, 1.8s)
    → QualityVideoAuditor → HunyuanVideo (DGX)
    → Topaz (optional 4K) → HLS/WebRTC stream → workspace
```

## Models

| Tier | Engine | Host |
|------|--------|------|
| Ultra-fast draft | LTX-Video, CogVideoX-5B | Mac M5 |
| Fast draft + lip-sync | Wan 2.2 | Mac M5 |
| Final | HunyuanVideo NVFP8 | DGX Spark |

## Orchestration

ComfyUI executes graphs; `supernova_video_runner.py` wires Pipecat-style processors before queueing jobs.

## Sovereign law

No cloud inference. Reference images and prompts never leave the home network.

articles.title: Ports, doors, health probes
articles.lede: http://video.localhost/ and workspace :8820 — never :8088 as UI.
articles.body:
**Operator copy:** use **http://<id>.localhost/** — run `make doors` once on a new Mac (sudo).

## Video doors

| Door | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| http://pockit.localhost/#/c/video | Video pad in Pockit shell |
| http://127.0.0.1:8820/ | Cinematic workspace (dev) |
| http://video.localhost/ | Video projection (planned) |
| http://video.localhost/super-rick | COMP bridge status |

## API (not a browser UI)

| Port | Service |
|------|---------|
| **8088** | tower-api — `/api/v1/video/*` JSON only |

## Debug (gateway port)

| Probe | Command |
|-------|---------|
| Video health | `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/v1/video/health` |
| Super Rick Video | `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/v1/video/super-rick/status` |
| HLS stub | `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/v1/video/stream/preview-stub/index.m3u8` |

## ComfyUI (existing cassette)

http://comfyui.localhost/ — node graphs; Super Rick Video orchestration layers on top (Bridge Plan).

articles.title: Streaming + Pockit / iOS integration
articles.lede: HLS and WebRTC preview from DGX; Voice handoff stub.
articles.body:
Progressive preview from DGX → MacBook / iOS while finals encode.

## Transport modes

| Mode | Latency | Client |
|------|---------|--------|
| **HLS** | ~2–4s segment | Safari, iOS WebView, workspace Preview tab |
| **WebRTC** | sub-500ms target | Mac workspace, iOS native player (slice 2) |

## Progressive chunks

1. Mac runs **DraftVideoGenerator** — sub-90s 720p loop.
2. Operator sends job to DGX — HunyuanVideo encodes in chunks.
3. Stream sidecar publishes manifest: `/api/v1/video/stream/:jobId/index.m3u8`.
4. Workspace `PreviewPane` attaches via `lib/streaming.ts`.

## WebRTC SFU (slice 2)

| Piece | Path / env |
|-------|------------|
| Signaling | `POST /api/v1/video/webrtc/offer` · `GET /api/v1/video/webrtc/config` |
| SFU health | `VIDEO_WEBRTC_SFU_URL` (default `http://127.0.0.1:8822`) — mediasoup/go2rtc on DGX (slice 3 deploy) |
| Workspace client | `apps/super-rick-video/lib/streaming.ts` — `RTCPeerConnection` + tower-api SDP exchange |
| Honest gate | When SFU is down, status LED shows **yellow**; HLS fallback remains available |

## Pockit App (first-class)

| Surface | Integration |
|---------|-------------|
| `#/c/video` | Console view — `video-rail-info.js` rails |
| Video.app accessory | Opens projection door (slice 2 — `make install-video-app`) |
| iOS | SwiftUI shell + HLS `AVPlayer` or WebRTC `RTCPeerConnection` stub |

## Voice handoff

One-tap **Send to Super Rick Video** from Super Rick Voice:

- `POST /api/v1/video/handoff/from-voice` (slice 2)
- Carries: last transcript, optional `[ref:]` still from voice session
- Opens workspace Refine tab with pre-filled prompt

## WireGuard remote

Away from LAN: same as Voice — wg0 tunnel to `10.1.0.5`, stream over encrypted mesh.

articles.title: Super Rick Video — what it is
articles.lede: Private local video generation on Mac M5 + DGX Spark — zero cloud.
articles.body:
**Super Rick Video** is the family's private local video generation platform — not a cloud renderer.

## Intent (from mac-app-intent-registry)

- **Why:** We built Super Rick Video because we refused cloud video tools that leak prompts, reference images, and family likenesses — fully private 8–30s cinematic clips on Family Office hardware.
- **Problem:** Cloud video APIs leak creative work, drift identity frame-to-frame, and expose node graphs operators shouldn't need.
- **Does:** Hybrid Mac M5 drafts (Wan 2.2, CogVideoX-5B, LTX-Video) + DGX HunyuanVideo finals, ComfyUI orchestration, Visual Consistency Swarmer, HLS/WebRTC preview, optional Topaz 4K.

Open the **Intent** pill on `#/c/video` for the full master document: success criteria + 10-row why guide (COMP v1.0.0).

## Surfaces

| Surface | URL | Role |
|---------|-----|------|
| Video pad (console) | http://pockit.localhost/#/c/video | Ops console — rails, deploy, stream |
| Cinematic workspace | http://127.0.0.1:8820/ | Preview · Final Render · Refine |
| Video projection | http://video.localhost/ | Standalone door (planned) |
| Super Rick status | http://video.localhost/super-rick | COMP bridge LED table |

## Cross-cassette

**Send to Super Rick Video** from Voice pad (slice 2) — transcript + reference still from last turn.

## North-star docs

| Doc | Role |
|-----|------|
| COMP v1.0.0 | Full target spec + seed |
| Bridge Plan | Evolve ComfyUI in place |
| Plan 0430 | Super Rick Video slices |
| `docs/super-rick-video-seed/` | Processor propagation |

## Success criteria

- 8–30s clips under 90s fast path (Mac draft)
- 1080p default / 4K final with Topaz post
- Temporal consistency via Visual Consistency Swarmer (1.8s agent timeout)
- Lip-sync when audio track present
- 100% local — no data leaves home network

articles.title: Cinematic workspace UI
articles.lede: Preview · Final Render · Refine tabs in apps/super-rick-video.
articles.body:
`apps/super-rick-video/` — Next.js 15 minimal workspace.

## Tabs

| Tab | Purpose |
|-----|---------|
| **Preview** | HLS/WebRTC live player — DGX progressive chunks |
| **Final Render** | HunyuanVideo job status, 1080p/4K export |
| **Refine** | Prompt + `[ref:path]` reference board before draft |

## Dev

```bash
cd apps/super-rick-video && pnpm install && pnpm dev
# http://127.0.0.1:8820/
```

## Docker

```bash
make video-seed
# or docs/super-rick-video-seed/plant-seed.sh
```

## Pockit-native

Embed via `#/c/video` console view — strips chrome with `pockit_hud=1` when iframe-wired (slice 2).

Design: near-black cinematic shell, cotton-ball tab transitions (Framer Motion), no exposed node graph.

articles.title: Help Command Center
articles.lede: Buttons instead of typing make commands — the operator console at console.localhost.
articles.body: Every Makefile target with a ## description appears in the catalog. Tap Play to run; read ⓘ before Boss Moves. Family shortcuts mirror Desktop .apps.
steps: make operator-console or open http://console.localhost/
Register SSH keys before Boss Moves.
Use Forge ship from Boss section to verify → PR → merge.

articles.title: Cassette quality gate
articles.lede: Before claiming a cassette is done, run the assembly line check.
articles.body: **make cassette-line CHECK=<id>** validates door URL, substrate fields, operator catalog entries, and vanilla shape. Embed apps (Gitea, Matrix) have their own SOP.
steps: Edit cassette → make cassette-line CHECK=<id>
Fix red lines in the log.
Ship with forge-ship on a feature branch.

articles.title: AI agents in Pockit
articles.lede: Copy agent context from any console cassette for Cursor, Claude, or Grok.
articles.body: Route FAB → Copy agent context bundles rules, catalog, and cassette fields. CLOAK MCP loads session memory via nephew_session_load. Expert autonomy means agents run safe commands themselves.
steps: Open a cassette → Copy agent context.
Paste into a new Cursor chat on Nephew repo.
Agents work in isolated worktrees — never shared main checkout.

articles.title: Forge — Gitea is origin
articles.lede: DGX Gitea is the canonical origin; GitHub/GitLab are mirrors. Ship via Gitea MR.
articles.body: Our source of truth is **Gitea on the DGX** (10.1.0.5:2424), backed to the NAS DXP6800 Pro. The git remote **`origin` points at Gitea**; **`github`/`gitlab`** are mirror remotes (backup only — never wait on GitHub Actions). A `gitea` alias keeps legacy `gitea/main` refs working. Branch from `origin/main` → open the **Gitea MR** → verify gate → squash-merge. Full mental model: **Nephew/Understandings/Forge/0001-gitea-canonical-origin.md**.
steps: Flip a host: bash scripts/flip-remotes-to-gitea-origin.sh (DRY_RUN=0 to apply).
Ship: branch from origin/main → Gitea MR → verify → squash-merge.
Mirror (optional): git push github main / git push gitlab main.
After soul/notice changes: make soul-sync on the DGX.

articles.title: Open Visual Obsidian
articles.lede: Visual is the sovereign Obsidian wiki on NAS — graphs, Understandings, and Visual-Home. Jarvis is voice only.
articles.body: The vault lives at **/Volumes/historia/nephew-sovereign-vault** (marker file **NEPHEW.md** at vault root). Agents and operators run **make visual-obsidian** — it mounts NAS if needed, syncs the agent routing graph, registers the vault, and opens **Visual-Home**. Use **make visual-obsidian-full** when you also need repo mirror rsync + cassette wiki export.
steps: make nas-mounts — or make install-nas-mounts once for auto-mount at login.
make visual-obsidian — opens Visual-Home in Obsidian.
Bookmark Visual-Home; use 03-Wiki/ for family knowledge.
Agents: never manual vault switching — run make visual-obsidian.

articles.title: NAS — historia auto-mount
articles.lede: /Volumes/historia must be mounted before Obsidian, vault sync, or WP mirror jobs run.
articles.body: **make install-nas-mounts** installs a LaunchAgent that mounts the required **historia** SMB share at login and every 5 minutes when home LAN is up. Registry: **data/nas-mac-mounts.json**. UGREEN NAS uses SMB port **74**. If Finder prompts once, enable Remember password + Reconnect at login.
steps: make install-nas-mounts (one-time per Mac).
make nas-mounts — manual mount or verify now.
Verify: mount | grep historia and test -d /Volumes/historia/nephew-sovereign-vault.
Logs: tail ~/.nephew/logs/nas-mounts.err.log (should stay empty).

articles.title: Understandings → vault + RAG
articles.lede: Git source of truth in Nephew/Understandings/ syncs to Obsidian and indexes into Brain A.
articles.body: Topic folders: **Pockit/**, **Voice/**, **Application/**, **Hardware/**, **Obsidian/**. **make vault-sync-understandings** copies them to **03-Wiki/Understandings/** in the sovereign vault. **make vault-ingest-docs** runs sync + live graph + incremental Qdrant index (**nephew-vault** on Spark). Mac syncs files; Spark runs Qdrant when local :6333 is not up.
steps: Edit docs under Nephew/Understandings/<topic>/.
make nas-mounts (historia mounted).
make vault-ingest-docs on Mac for vault sync; on Spark for full Qdrant index.
Open Visual-Home → browse 03-Wiki/Understandings/.

articles.title: LiveSync — sovereign vault sync
articles.lede: Self-hosted LiveSync against CouchDB on Spark — no iCloud, no third-party cloud.
articles.body: Device creds live in **~/.nephew/livesync-fivemac.env** (never commit). E2EE passphrase in **~/.nephew/livesync-e2e.env**. Tunnel: **http://127.0.0.1:5984/nephew-sovereign**. **Do not use Setup URI** on FIVEMAC unless cloning from another device that already syncs — use manual server info or **make visual-obsidian-repair** instead.
steps: make livesync-setup — first device: CouchDB on Spark + device user + config.
In Obsidian: Connection Method → Enter server information manually.
URI http://127.0.0.1:5984/nephew-sovereign · user/pass from ~/.nephew/livesync-fivemac.env.
E2EE ON — passphrase from ~/.nephew/livesync-e2e.env · save to Keychain.
Test connection → Initialize database (first device) or Replicate from server.
Or skip wizard: make visual-obsidian-repair (writes config + Replicate now).

articles.title: Stuck on Setup URI?
articles.lede: Cancel that dialog — Setup URI is for copying settings from another device, not first-time FIVEMAC setup.
articles.body: The red **obsidian://setuplivesync?settings=…** field is invalid unless pasted from a phone or Mac that already syncs. On this Mac you already have sovereign creds. **Cancel** → pick **Enter server information manually**, or quit Obsidian and run **make visual-obsidian-repair** to apply config from ~/.nephew automatically. Wrong E2EE passphrase corrupts server ciphertext — always use the passphrase from **~/.nephew/livesync-e2e.env**.
steps: Click Cancel on Enter Setup URI.
Run make visual-obsidian-repair from ~/Developer/nephew (recommended).
Or manual: URI http://127.0.0.1:5984/nephew-sovereign + creds from livesync-fivemac.env.
Cmd+Shift+P → Replicate now if upload has not started.
Vault routing doc: 07-Agent-Routing/LiveSync-Sovereign-Setup.md on NAS.

articles.title: Door 502 or blank page
articles.lede: Gateway cannot reach the tape backend.
articles.body: Check announce files and family-health. Often fixed by make doors, make pockit, or restarting tower-api.
steps: make doors-verify — read the log.
make family-status — confirm gateway + tower.
curl http://pockit.localhost/ with Host header if debugging.

articles.title: tower-api offline
articles.lede: Voice, RAG, and auth need tower-api on :8088 (API only — use tower-api.localhost door for UI).
articles.body: make ensure-tower-api or make tower-doctor. Register SSH keys if SSO cookies fail.
steps: make tower-doctor
make family-ssh-login
Retry the cassette that called the API.

articles.title: Version looks wrong
articles.lede: Footer version should match CHANGELOG and origin/main after merge.
articles.body: Hard refresh the browser. If TLD apex is stale but local doors are current, deploy edge (Clinic 0045). Agents must read pockit-surface.json from disk every turn.
steps: Compare footer pill to docs/CHANGELOG.md top entry.
git pull on the host serving family-hub.
Shift-reload http://pockit.localhost/

articles.title: Historia / vault not mounted
articles.lede: Visual Obsidian and vault-ingest fail when /Volumes/historia is missing.
articles.body: Run **make nas-mounts**. If it keeps failing, run **make install-nas-mounts** to refresh the LaunchAgent. Off home LAN the script skips quietly. On LAN, check ~/.nephew/logs/nas-mounts.err.log. Boss Move: Finder → smb://abrownsanta@192.168.10.119:74/historia with Remember password.
steps: make nas-mounts
test -d /Volumes/historia/nephew-sovereign-vault/NEPHEW.md
make install-nas-mounts if LaunchAgent is missing or stale.
Retry make visual-obsidian or make vault-ingest-docs.
